⚠ Missouri no hands laws — pull over to use
⚙️ Settings
Tap any panel to expand. All changes save when you tap Save & Close.

Upload your city ID card. Use rotation buttons to orient correctly.

YOUR NAME / BADGE ID
Used in CSB clipboard and completion logs.
WORK EMAIL

Choosing a position pre-fills title, division, department, scope, and the matching contacts — and sets defaults for Roles, Summary & Ordinance pages.

Mirrors the action row on stop cards so the Navigate button sits on the left (thumb-side for left-handed grip).

TITLE / ROLE

🔑 Anthropic API Key
Required for photo scanning — console.anthropic.com · Encrypted on device with your PIN
Not set — photo scan unavailable
PIN protects your key — you'll need it each session. Don't lose it.
📊 AI Usage (this session)
Token counts for requests made with your key. Session resets on refresh; Today, 7d, and 30d are persisted on-device.
No AI calls yet this session.
🎤 Voice Note Cleanup
When you dictate a stop note via the mic button, run the raw transcript through Claude to fix punctuation, expand spoken numbers, and correct mishearings of common St. Louis street names. Original raw text is kept so you can undo.
Off by default. Requires an Anthropic API key. Each cleanup uses a small amount of your usage budget — typically < 1¢ per note.
📷 Photo Cache (48h)
A temporary in-app copy of every photo the app captures — stamped photos, photoscan camera images, and custom-layer scan photos all share this pool. The total respects the same size cap and auto-deletes after 48 hours. Your phone gallery keeps permanent copies regardless.
Loading…
When ON, the camera image you took before AI extracted addresses is offered through your phone's share sheet (save to gallery, send to Drive, etc). Off by default for privacy and storage.
⏰ Fiscal-Day Cutoff & Archive
Once a fiscal day rolls over, completed stops from earlier days move out of your active list and into the archive. Pending stops stay in the list. Set the cutoff to whatever marks the END of your work day — anything completed AFTER this time is treated as the next fiscal day's work.
Archive empty.
✨ Nearby-Stops Highlight Radius
When two or more pending stops sit within this radius, each of them gets a gold border in the Stops, Route, and Map tabs — so you can spot natural clusters at a glance.
Default: 2 blocks. Set higher to see more matches; lower for only the closest pairs.
📧 How email attachments work

Short version: the app keeps a temporary 48-hour cache of stamped photos so the 📧 Email + Photos button can attach them to email automatically. After 48 hours the cached copies are deleted to keep the app small. Your phone gallery always keeps the permanent copies (those are saved by Download / Share, never the cache).

Why a cache and not unlimited storage? Web apps (which is what this is, technically — a PWA) live in a sandbox the browser enforces. The sandbox lets the app hand photos to your phone (via the Share sheet or Download) but never lets it read back photos from your gallery. So once a photo leaves the cache, the app can\u2019t pull it back to attach. Storing them indefinitely in-app would slowly fill your phone\u2019s storage and on iOS would risk being silently wiped by the system. 48 hours is the sweet spot — long enough to cover Friday\u2192Monday workflows, short enough to stay tiny.

What to do in each case:

  • 📷 Just took photos: tap 📧 Email Summary + Photos in the photo preview, OR mark the stop done and tap 📧📷 Email Summary + photos attached in the close-stop screen. Either way your share sheet opens with the stop summary text and all the stamped photos pre-attached.
  • 📅 Within 48 hours: open the stop, tap ✅ MARK COMPLETE (or just tap the existing complete stop), the close-stop modal will show the 📧📷 Email Summary + photos attached button if cached photos still exist for this stop. The cache count appears under the button.
  • 📝 Older than 48 hours: the 📧 Email Stop Summary button still sends the full text summary, and the email body lists the exact filenames of every photo taken at that stop — so you (or the recipient) know which files to attach manually from the gallery.
  • 📱 Filenames: photos are named like 2801daltonavenue042326101832847.jpg (address + date + time + ms). Search for that filename in your gallery or file manager.

Cache size limits: normally 200MB. On phones with less than ~150MB free, the cap drops to 50MB to be polite. Either way, when full, the oldest photos go first. The newest photo for any stop is always preserved as long as it fits.

The cache is a working convenience, not a permanent record. The photos you see in your phone\u2019s Photos app or Downloads folder are the real archive — the app never modifies or removes those.

📈 Google Sheets Sync
Paste the Apps Script Web App URL to enable "Push to Sheets" from the Data tab. One-time setup — follow steps below.
Not configured
📋 Setup steps + Apps Script code
1. Open a new Google Sheet at sheets.google.com
2. Click Extensions → Apps Script
3. Delete the existing code and paste the script below
4. Click Deploy → New deployment → Web app
5. Set "Who has access" to Anyone
6. Click Deploy and copy the Web App URL
7. Paste the URL above and tap SAVE URL 
function doPost(e) {
  var data = JSON.parse(e.postData.contents);
  var ss = SpreadsheetApp.getActiveSpreadsheet();
  var sh = ss.getSheetByName('Stops') || ss.insertSheet('Stops');
  if (sh.getLastRow() === 0)
    sh.appendRow(['Date','Address','Type','Notes','Done','DurationMins','Lat','Lng','Inspector']);
  (data.stops || []).forEach(s => sh.appendRow([
    data.exportedAt, s.address, s.type,
    s.notes||'', s.done, s.durationMins||'',
    s.lat||'', s.lng||'', data.inspector
  ]));
  return ContentService.createTextOutput('ok');
}
📱 App Install & GPS
Checking…
GPS works after installing
✅ BEST — Free HTTPS in 2 minutes (works on any device)
1. The app is already hosted at:
   sprightly-brigadeiros-d02253.netlify.app
2. Open that URL in Chrome (Android) or Safari (iOS)
3. Chrome: tap ⋮ menu → "Add to Home Screen"
   Safari: tap Share ⬆ → "Add to Home Screen"
4. Open app from home screen icon → GPS works ✓

To update the hosted app after a new version:
   Go to app.netlify.com/drop and drag the new HTML file
🤖 ANDROID — Add to home screen from Chrome
After opening the Netlify URL in Chrome:
• Tap the three dots ⋮ in the top-right corner
• Tap "Add to Home screen"
• Tap "Add" in the popup
• You'll see the app icon on your home screen
• GPS works every time you open it from that icon
🍎 iPHONE/iPAD — Must use Safari (not Chrome)
After opening the Netlify URL in Safari:
• Tap the Share button ⬆ at the bottom of the screen
• Scroll down and tap "Add to Home Screen"
• Tap "Add" in the top-right corner
• GPS works every time you open it from that icon
Note: Chrome on iPhone cannot install home screen apps — use Safari
📷 Photo Stamp
What gets burned into the corner of every stamped photo.
🏷️ Custom Stop Types
Added from the stop type dropdown on the add-stop panel. Max 10. Deleting reverts stops to "Other".
🔘 Header Buttons
Show or hide the clock-in icon in the header. Off by default.
👤 Profiles
Use multiple profiles when one phone is shared between job roles or inspectors. Each profile has its own stops, ID photo, settings, and API key. Switching profiles reloads the app.
A new profile starts empty — the setup wizard runs again so you can configure it. Existing profiles are not affected.
💾 Profile Backup & Restore
Saves the currently-active profile to a single file you can email yourself or stash in cloud storage. When you get a new phone, restore the file to bring all your stops, settings, ID card, custom map layers, and history with you.
Included: stops, route order, settings, ID photo, custom stop types, recipients, custom map layers (geocoded addresses), photo log filenames, daily metrics history.
Excluded: photo bytes (kept on your phone storage), encrypted API key (re-enter on the new device), session-only flags.
🙏 Help Improve the App
Opt in to share anonymous usage pings with the developer. Pings include: install status, app open events, and which features you use. No names, addresses, or photos are ever sent. Off by default.
You can turn this off at any time. Each ping is a single tiny POST to a Google Apps Script, then discarded.
🛠 Diagnostics
When something silently doesn't work, recent absorbed errors may show up here. The buffer holds the most recent 100 entries and clears on app reload.
Tap REFRESH to view recent absorbed errors.
⚠ Danger Zone
RESET erases every piece of data this app has stored on your phone — stops, route history, custom map layers, completed metrics, settings, ID photo, encrypted API key, everything. Next time you open the app the setup wizard will run from scratch.

Export your profile first if you want any of this data back. There is no undo.
📥 Import Profile
Reading file…
As a new profile (recommended)
Creates a new profile with the file's data. Your current profile is not changed. Switches to the new profile after import.
Or merge into current profile
Adds stops, archives, transactions, custom types, and map layers from the file. Inspector identity, ID photo, API key, preferences, and webhook URLs are kept as-is on this device (additive only — never overwritten).
📦 Stop Archive
Loading…
✨ Supercharge with AI
Add an Anthropic API key to unlock smart features that work right on your phone:
📷 Photo address scan — snap a printed route sheet, AI reads the addresses
🎤 Voice note cleanup — dictate a stop note, AI fixes punctuation + St. Louis street names
🌅 End-of-day debrief — get a summary of patterns, hot-spots, and tomorrow's priorities
💬 Ask your data — natural-language questions about your stops
🎨 Custom layer scan — photograph a list, build a map layer from it
How to get a key:
1. Visit console.anthropic.com in your browser
2. Sign up (free) and add a small starting balance ($5 lasts most users a long time)
3. Tap "API Keys" → "Create Key" → copy the sk-ant-… string
4. Paste it below with a 4+ digit PIN to encrypt it
Your key is encrypted with this PIN before being stored on your device. The unencrypted key never leaves your phone except when calling Anthropic. Don't lose the PIN — there's no recovery.

💾 Stored History
St. Louis City Employment Opportunities ↗
🗺️
YourCityRoute
FIELD WORKER GUIDE
🧭 What is YourCityRoute?
YourCityRoute is a mobile route planner built for field workers who visit multiple addresses in a single shift — city inspectors, code enforcement officers, pest control techs, utility workers, property managers, and anyone else working a stop list.

No login. No server. All data stays on your device. Works offline once loaded. Install it to your home screen for the best experience.
🚀 Getting Started (5 minutes)
1. Set up your profile — Tap ⚙️ (top right) → enter your name, title, division, department, and contact info. This appears on your ID card and in all exports.

2. Select your position — In the same Settings screen, choose your role from the "Select Your Position" dropdown. This auto-fills your profile and pre-loads your Roles, Ordinances, and Summary pages.

3. Add your first stop — Go to the STOPS tab, type an address, and tap ADD. The app will geocode it and add it to your list.

4. Optimize your route — Go to the ROUTE tab and tap ⚡ OPTIMIZE. The app uses your GPS location and a nearest-neighbor algorithm to sort stops for minimum driving time.

5. Clock in — Tap ⏱ IN at the top of the screen when your shift starts. This enables shift tracking, break time, and accurate metrics.
📋 STOPS Tab
Adding stops:
· Type an address in the input field and tap ADD (or press Enter)
· 🎤 Voice — speak addresses one at a time; pause between each
· 📷 Photo scan — photograph a printed route sheet; AI reads the addresses (requires Anthropic API key in Settings)
· 📋 Paste — copy a list of addresses from any source and paste it
· 📊 Spreadsheet — upload a CSV or Excel file with addresses in the first column

Stop types: Tap the color badge on any stop to cycle through Inspection · Service Request · Work Order · General. You can also create custom types in Settings.

Stop buttons (left to right):
· 📝 Notes — add or view field notes for this stop
· 🔍 Property search — opens City property lookup for this address
· CSB — copies address and opens the Citizens Service Bureau request form
· 📍 ARRIVE — tap when you pull up to the stop; starts a dwell timer
· ✓ CLOSE — mark the stop complete; prompts for completion notes and offers email options (see Email Stop Summaries below)
· 🧭 (round, right) — navigate to this address via Google Maps

Photo capture per stop: Tap and hold the photostamp button (📷 in the top header) while a stop is active to start a photo session linked to that stop. Each stamped photo is saved to your phone gallery AND cached in the app for 48 hours so you can email them with the stop summary later. See the Photo Stamping & Cache and Email Stop Summaries sections below for details.

Completed stops collapse to a separate "Completed" section at the bottom. Use ↩ to restore or 🗑 to delete permanently.

Duplicate detection: Adding an address already on the list shows a prompt — tap to confirm or cancel.
🛣️ ROUTE Tab
⚡ Optimize Route Order — Sorts your pending stops using your current GPS location + nearest-neighbor + 2-opt improvement. A drive time estimate appears after.

📌 Pin a start — Tap 📌 on any stop to lock it as your first stop regardless of optimization.

↺ Reset Order — Returns stops to the order they were added.

▲▼ Reorder — Tap the arrows on any route card to manually bump a stop up or down in priority.

Route AI Narrative — After optimizing, if you have an Anthropic API key set, the app generates a brief plain-English description of the route sequence.

Navigate all — Tap 🗺️ in the route tab header to open the full multi-stop route in Google Maps (up to 10 waypoints).
🗺️ MAP Tab
Shows all pending stops as numbered pins on an interactive map. Tap a pin to see the address and access Navigate, Property Search, and Close from the map popup.

The map auto-fits to your stops when it first loads. Pinch to zoom, drag to pan. Completed stops are hidden; active stops show in route order.

If your stops are geocoded (most are), they appear immediately. Stops added by address alone may take a moment to place while the app looks up coordinates.
📷 Photo Stamping & Cache
What is photostamping? The 📷 button in the top header opens your camera and overlays the address, date, time, and inspector name onto the bottom-right corner of every photo you take. The stamped JPEG saves to your phone's gallery (iOS) or Downloads folder (Android) automatically.

Per-stop photo sessions: When you launch the camera from a specific stop's row, the photo is linked to that stop. Each stamped shot increments a 📷 counter on the stop card so you can see at a glance how many photos you've taken at that address.

Filenames are searchable: Stamped photos are named like 2801daltonavenue042326101832847.jpg — that's address + date (mmddyy) + time (hhmmss) + milliseconds. You can search this exact name in your Photos / Files app to find any photo later.

The 48-hour cache: A copy of every stamped photo is also kept inside the app for 48 hours. This is what makes the 📧 Email Summary + photos attached button work for stops you photographed earlier in the day or yesterday. After 48 hours the cached copy is auto-deleted to keep the app small — your phone gallery copies are not affected and remain forever.

Cache size: Capped at 200MB normally, or 50MB on phones with limited free space. When the cache fills up, oldest photos go first; the newest photo for each stop is always preserved.

Manage the cache: Settings → 📷 Photo Cache (48h) shows current size and a "🗑 Clear Cache Now" button. Clearing the cache only affects in-app copies — gallery photos are untouched.

Quick mode: Tap the ⚡ toggle in the photo preview to auto-dismiss the preview and reopen the camera after each shot. Good for batch capture (multiple shots at one stop).

Settings → 📷 Stamp Settings lets you control what gets burned into the stamp (address, date, time, inspector name, GPS coords) and the size (small / medium / large).
📧 Email Stop Summaries
What's in a stop summary email? Address, type, times (added / arrived / completed / dwell), GPS coordinates, your inspector name, completion notes, transactions with totals, and the filenames of any photos taken at that stop.

Three ways to send one:
· 📧 EMAIL STOP SUMMARY in the close-stop modal (text only) — opens your share sheet or mail app with the summary pre-filled. Photo filenames are listed in the body so the recipient knows what to look for in the gallery.
· 📧📷 EMAIL SUMMARY + photos attached in the close-stop modal (text + photos) — appears only if photos for this stop are still in the 48-hour cache. Opens the share sheet with the stamped JPEGs pre-attached.
· 📧 EMAIL SUMMARY + PHOTOS in the photo preview modal — uses the photos from the active session you just captured. Always works as long as you haven't tapped Done yet.

Why two versions of the email? Web apps can't read photos from your gallery (browser security rule). The 48-hour cache works around this by keeping in-app copies just long enough to cover the typical email-after-stop window. Past 48 hours the email body still lists every filename — useful so you (or the recipient) can manually attach from the gallery.

Cross-platform compatibility: The summary uses CRLF line endings so it formats correctly in Gmail composer on Android. The mailto: fallback uses an anchor click instead of a navigation, so PWA users on iOS don't lose their session opening Mail.

See Settings → 📧 How email attachments work for the full explainer.
📍 Location Metadata & Follow-Up Flag
Neighborhood & ward auto-detection. When a stop is added with a verified address, the app silently looks up which St. Louis neighborhood (1 of 79) and ward the address falls in. The result shows up as a small green line under the address: e.g. 📍 Tower Grove South #15 · Ward 8. Useful for grouping stops by district when you're filing reports or coordinating with ward leaders.

Manual edit. If the auto-lookup is wrong or unavailable (offline first-fetch, etc.), open ✎ Edit Stop from the stop card's utility menu and you can override the neighborhood name, neighborhood number (1–79), and ward number directly. Manual values stick; subsequent auto-lookups won't overwrite them.

Photostamp inclusion. Settings → 📷 Photo Stamp now has three new toggles: Neighborhood Name, Neighborhood Number, and Ward Number. Turn any combination on to burn the location info into the stamp on each photo. Compact form: "Tower Grove South #15 · Ward 8".

🔔 Complete & Flag for Follow-Up. The close-stop modal now has two action buttons: the regular ✅ MARK COMPLETE and a new ✅🔔 COMPLETE & FLAG FOR FOLLOW-UP. Use the flag variant when a stop technically closes today but you know it'll need another visit (owner agreed to fix by date X, scheduled re-inspection, called CSB about a complaint). The reason field above the buttons is optional — fill it if you want context, leave it blank if you just want the flag.

Where the flag shows up:
· A bright yellow 🔔 banner on the completed stop's card
· A prominent banner at the top of the email summary body
· 🔔 prefix on the email subject line
· A boolean column in the Google Sheets push payload (visible to anyone reviewing the sheet)
· Editable through ✎ Edit Stop — uncheck the flag if no longer needed
🪪 INFO Tab
ID Card — Your digital business card. Shows your name, title, division, department, scope, email, and phone. Tap it to show a fullscreen version. Upload an ID photo via ⚙️ Settings.

❓ Questions & Follow-Up — Contact information for departments you selected in Settings. CSB is always first. One-tap dialing.

⚖️ Roles & Authority — Three panels (Roles, Summary, Ordinance) loaded from your saved position. Use the position selector to preview any other role without changing your profile. Navigate away and your saved default restores automatically.

📄 Reference Documents — Upload and store images (maps, forms, permit templates, reference sheets) for quick in-field lookup.

📋 RESOURCES — Local contacts, city departments, wildlife rescue, property lookup tools, pest control, and more.

📊 Data & Metrics — Your shift data, stop history, and export tools.

❓ Help & Guide — This page.

⚙️ Settings — Includes:
  · Profile (name, title, division, contact info, ID photo)
  · Position selector + custom stop types
  · 📷 Stamp Settings (what gets burned into photos)
  · Anthropic API Key (encrypted with PIN)
  · 📊 AI Usage (per-feature token counts, this session)
  · 📷 Photo Cache (48h temporary photo storage management)
  · 📧 How email attachments work (explainer)
  · 📈 Google Sheets Sync (one-time setup for push-to-sheets)
  · App Install (PWA add-to-home-screen helper)
  · Share link (send the app URL to a coworker)
⏱ Clock & Shift Metrics
Clock In/Out — Tap ⏱ IN at the start of your shift. The elapsed work time appears below the button. Tap ⏱ OUT to end the shift.

☕ Break — While clocked in, a BREAK button appears. Tap it to pause the work timer. Your break time is tracked separately. Tap RESUME to continue.

Metrics tracked per shift:
· Stops completed and close rate
· Stops per hour and average minutes per stop
· First and last stop times
· Dwell time at stops (from ARRIVE → CLOSE)
· Return visits (same address visited more than once)
· Unique streets covered
· Overtime flag (shift > 8 hours worked)

All shift data is stored locally and included in your exports. Open the DATA tab to view and export.
📊 Data & Exports
Open the DATA tab from the INFO screen to see today's metrics and your shift history.

Date ranges: Today · 7 Days · 30 Days · All Time · Custom.

Export formats:
· CSV Spreadsheet — One row per stop. Columns: #, Inspector, Address, Type, Status, AddedAt, CompletedAt, DurationMins, Lat, Lng, Notes. Opens in Excel or Google Sheets.
· Text Summary — Three-section plain-text report (Field Worker, Manager, Geographic). Good for email.
· Push to Sheets — Sends data directly to a Google Sheet you control. Requires one-time Apps Script setup (see below).

Auto-send: Enable in the DATA tab to have a daily summary emailed automatically at a time you set.
📈 Google Sheets Setup
One-time setup — takes about 5 minutes:

1. Open a new Google Sheet at sheets.google.com
2. Click Extensions → Apps Script
3. Delete the existing code and paste the script below
4. Click Deploy → New deployment → Web app
5. Set "Who has access" to Anyone
6. Click Deploy and copy the Web App URL
7. In this app, tap Export → Push to Sheets → paste the URL

After setup, every time you tap "Push to Sheets" your latest stops and metrics are appended to your sheet automatically. 
function doPost(e) {
  var data = JSON.parse(e.postData.contents);
  var ss = SpreadsheetApp.getActiveSpreadsheet();
  var sh = ss.getSheetByName('Stops') || ss.insertSheet('Stops');
  if (sh.getLastRow() === 0)
    sh.appendRow(['Date','Address','Type','Notes','Done','DurationMins','Lat','Lng','Inspector']);
  (data.stops || []).forEach(s => sh.appendRow([
    data.exportedAt, s.address, s.type,
    s.notes||'', s.done, s.durationMins||'',
    s.lat||'', s.lng||'', data.inspector
  ]));
  return ContentService.createTextOutput('ok');
}
🔧 Toolbar Buttons
These appear at the top of every screen:

· ⚖️ CASE — Opens Missouri Case.net (22nd Judicial Circuit) for municipal court case lookups
· 📞 CSB — Opens the Citizens Service Bureau. Copies the current stop's address to clipboard first if one is active
· 🔍 Search — City of St. Louis property lookup by address
· 📷 Photostamp — Opens your camera with stop-aware overlay. Stamped photos save to your gallery and to the 48-hour cache (see Photo Stamping & Cache section)
· ⚙️ (top right) — Opens your Settings / profile editor

Other resources (city departments, wildlife rescue, pest control, property lookup tools) are available in the Info tab under the 📋 RESOURCES button.
🤖 AI Features (Optional)
Four features unlock when you add a free Anthropic API key in Settings:

· 📷 Photo scan — Photograph a printed route sheet, dispatch list, or whiteboard. AI reads the addresses and adds them to your stop list.
· Address smart-complete — When you type a partial or ambiguous address, AI suggests the most likely full St. Louis address.
· Address list cleanup — When pasting or scanning a batch of addresses, AI normalizes formatting and corrects obvious typos.
· Route narrative — Plain-English description of your optimized route after you tap ⚡ OPTIMIZE.

Get a key: Visit console.anthropic.com, create a free account, and generate a key. Paste it into Settings → Anthropic API Key with a 4+ character PIN.

Security: Your key is encrypted on-device with AES-GCM using your PIN as the password. The encrypted blob lives in this app's private storage — it never leaves your phone unencrypted, and other apps on your phone can't see it.

Model: All AI requests use Claude Haiku (the fastest, cheapest tier). The app intentionally won't use more expensive models — this gives you predictable costs and limits damage if your key is ever leaked.

Usage tracking: Settings → 📊 AI Usage shows per-feature call counts and token usage for the current session, so you can see what your key has spent. Resets when you reload the app.
💡 Tips & Best Practices
· Clock in before adding stops so your shift metrics are accurate from the first address.
· Tap 📍 ARRIVE when you pull up to a stop — this starts a dwell timer so you know how long each visit took.
· Add notes at close — the CLOSE dialog has a notes field. Use it for condition observations, owner contact attempts, or case numbers.
· Pin your first stop on the ROUTE tab if you know you need to start at a specific address (e.g., a scheduled appointment).
· Email summaries with photos work for 48 hours. If you took photos at a stop today and want to email them tomorrow morning, the cached copies are still in the app — just open the stop and use the 📧📷 button. After 48 hours, the email body still lists every photo filename so you can attach manually from your gallery.
· Run quick mode for batch photos at multi-photo stops — toggle ⚡ in the photo preview and the camera reopens automatically after each shot.
· Install the app to your home screen via Settings → App Install for offline access and a faster experience.
· The map auto-updates when you switch to it after making changes — no need to refresh.
· Completed stops save permanently until you clear them. Your history is available under "All Time" in the DATA tab.
· Watch your AI spend in Settings → 📊 AI Usage if you're using photo scan heavily — image inputs are the most expensive type of request.
· Photo gallery is the permanent record. The app's 48-hour cache is convenience only. Photos in your phone's Photos / Downloads folder never expire.
📬 Contact & Support
Questions, feedback, bug reports, or partnership inquiries — reach us directly.
✉️ Email Support
support@yourcityroute.com

St. Louis City Employment Opportunities ↗
Notes
Notes are saved to this stop and included in exports.
📤 Export Data
Choose format and delivery method
DOWNLOAD
DELIVERY
📡 Location Unavailable
GPS requires either HTTPS or being saved as a PWA. Here are your options:
✅ BEST: Save to Home Screen
iOS Safari: tap Share → "Add to Home Screen"
Android Chrome: tap ⋮ → "Add to Home Screen"
GPS works when launched from home screen icon.
🌐 GOOD: Host on GitHub Pages (free)
1. Create free GitHub account
2. Upload this HTML file to a repo
3. Enable GitHub Pages → instant HTTPS URL
4. Bookmark that URL — GPS always works
📍 FALLBACK: IP Location (already tried)
City-block accuracy via your IP address.
Good enough for route planning when you're at your desk.
Not suitable for real-time field tracking.
⌨️ ALWAYS WORKS: Manual Entry
Type your starting address in the Route tab.
Works from any file or URL.
📷 Import Addresses
📋 How to Import Addresses
Change Stop Type
—
💰 Record Payment
—
1. OPEN PAYMENT APP (OPTIONAL)
Tap an app to launch it for the transaction. Come back after payment completes to log it.
2. LOG THE TRANSACTION
💳 Payment App Setup
Toggle apps you accept payments through, and enter your handle/username so the "OPEN APP" button pre-fills the recipient.
📷 Prepare Photo
Set the details that will be stamped onto this photo. You can edit them again after the shot.
Tip: tap Pick Existing and select multiple photos to stamp all of them with the same address.
📷 Stamped Photo
✎ Edit stamp (this shot only)
💾 Download saves the stamped photo to your phone's Downloads. 📤 Share opens the share sheet (email, Drive, Files, Photos, etc.).
Opens your share sheet with the stop summary and the photos from this session attached. Works only while this preview is open — once you tap Done, the photos stay in your gallery but can\u2019t be re-attached from inside the app.
📷 Stamp Settings
Choose what gets burned into the corner of every stamped photo.
Stamp size
How large the stamp text appears on your photo.
Stamp position
Where the stamp bar appears. Switch to top if your subjects are usually at the bottom of the frame.
✎ Edit Stop
Change the address, stop type, or initial notes on this stop.
📍 LOCATION METADATA
Auto-filled from STL GIS once the address has coordinates. Edit manually if the lookup is wrong or unavailable.
＋ Create Custom Stop Type
Add a custom category to the stop type dropdown. Delete or edit any time from Settings.
🗺️ Map Layers
Toggle layers on or off. Custom layers from a list of addresses can be added below.
Paste addresses · Upload CSV · Scan a printed list
Detailed parcel data · ArcGIS · Opens externally

📋 Resources
❤ Patreon
Tap any link to open · Phone numbers dial directly
This app is funded by local government sponsorships and listings on this page — no pop-up ads or banners. Just the information you need, where you know to look.
📣 List Your Business in Resources
Reach city field workers exactly when they need your service
Who sees your listing
STLCityRoute is used daily by City of St. Louis field inspectors — nuisance, building, housing, animal control, zoning, and environmental health. Your listing appears in the Resources panel, one tap away at the exact moment a worker needs to refer your type of service.
🌱
Nonprofits list free
Qualified 501(c)(3) nonprofits providing services relevant to city field work receive a complimentary Standard listing. Contact us with your EIN to apply. Subject to availability and annual review.
⭐ PREMIERE
Premiere Listing
Top of Resources page + top of category · alphabetical among Premiere
$150/yr
Your listing appears prominently at the top of the entire Resources page AND at the top of your category — above all Featured and Standard listings. Multiple Premiere listings are sorted alphabetically. Includes name, phone, website, and extended two-line description.
FEATURED
Featured Listing
Top of category (below Premiere) · alphabetical among Featured
$75/yr
Appears at the top of your category section, above all Standard listings and below any Premiere listings. Sorted alphabetically among other Featured businesses. Includes name, phone, website, and one-line description.
Standard Listing
Listed in category · alphabetical among Standard
$25/yr
Listed in your category, sorted alphabetically among Standard listings. Includes business name, phone number, website, and one-line description. One-tap dial for field workers.
ALL LISTINGS INCLUDE
📞 One-tap phone dial
🔗 Website link
📧 Up to 4 contacts
✏️ Free info updates
OPEN CATEGORIES
Pest ControlRodent, insect, vermin
Wildlife RemovalLicensed nuisance trapping
Property LawCode enforcement defense
Towing & RecoveryAbandoned vehicles
EnvironmentalAsbestos, mold, hazmat
Boarding & SecuringVacant structures
DemolitionLicensed demo contractors
Property RehabDistressed property work
Policy
St. Louis area businesses with services relevant to city field work. All listings reviewed before publication. Listings include: business name (linked to website), up to 10-word description, and up to 4 email/phone contacts. Advertising never influences official city contacts — paid listings appear only in the Local Business Listings section. Annual billing · invoice or Venmo · live within 48 hours of payment · lapsed listings removed and slot re-opens.
Ready to list your business?
Email us your business name, category, and preferred tier. We'll confirm availability and get you live within 48 hours.
📧 support@yourcityroute.com
